ucs.title: [LLM] Qilin / Agenda ransomware Safe-Mode reboot prep (bcdedit safeboot + SafeBoot\Network registry seeding)
ucs.description: Hunts Qilin (a.k.a. Agenda) ransomware's signature pre-encryption move — using bcdedit.exe to set the next boot to safeboot network/minimal and (often) registering its own service under HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\SafeBoot\Network so the encryptor survives the forced reboot into Safe Mode where most EDR/AV agents are inert. This chain is publicly documented as a Qilin.B / Agenda hallmark and is rare in benign IT activity.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name=bcdedit.exe (Processes.process="*safeboot*network*" OR Processes.process="*safeboot*minimal*") Processes.user!="*$" by host Processes.user Processes.process Processes.parent_process_name Processes.parent_process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename host as bcd_host, process as bcd_cmd, _time as bcd_time | join type=outer bcd_host [ | tstats summariesonly=true count as reg_writes min(_time) as reg_time from datamodel=Endpoint.Registry where Endpoint.Registry.registry_path="*\\Control\\SafeBoot\\Network*" by host Endpoint.Registry.registry_path Endpoint.Registry.registry_value_name Endpoint.Registry.registry_value_data Endpoint.Registry.process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Endpoint.Registry)` | rename host as bcd_host ] | where isnull(reg_time) OR abs(reg_time-firstTime) <= 3600 | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) ctime(reg_time)
ucs.kql:
let TimeWindow = 7d;
let SafeBootBcd = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(TimeWindow)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where FileName =~ "bcdedit.exe"
| where ProcessCommandLine has "safeboot"
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("network", "minimal")
| project BcdTimestamp = Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          BcdCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
          BcdParent = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          BcdParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine;
SafeBootBcd
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceRegistryEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(TimeWindow)
    | where ActionType in ("RegistryKeyCreated", "RegistryValueSet")
    | where RegistryKey has @"\Control\SafeBoot\Network"
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ ("TrustedInstaller.exe", "msiexec.exe")
    | project RegTimestamp = Timestamp, DeviceName, RegistryKey, RegistryValueName,
              RegistryValueData, RegProc = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              RegProcCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine
  ) on DeviceName
| where isnull(RegTimestamp) or abs(datetime_diff('minute', RegTimestamp, BcdTimestamp)) <= 60
| project BcdTimestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, BcdCmd, BcdParent, BcdParentCmd,
          RegTimestamp, RegistryKey, RegistryValueName, RegistryValueData, RegProc, RegProcCmd
| order by BcdTimestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let TimeWindow = 7d;
let SafeBootBcd = SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(TimeWindow)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| where NewProcessName endswith @"\bcdedit.exe"
| where CommandLine has "safeboot"
| where CommandLine has_any ("network", "minimal")
| project BcdTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, BcdCmd = CommandLine, ParentProcessName;
SafeBootBcd
| join kind=leftouter (
    Event
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(TimeWindow)
    | where EventLog == "Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational" and EventID == 13
    | extend TargetObject = tostring(parse_xml(EventData).DataItem.EventData.Data[4]["#text"])
    | where TargetObject has @"\Control\SafeBoot\Network"
    | project RegTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, TargetObject
  ) on Computer
| where isnull(RegTime) or abs(datetime_diff('minute', RegTime, BcdTime)) <= 60
| project BcdTime, Computer, Account, BcdCmd, ParentProcessName, RegTime, TargetObject
| order by BcdTime desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Qilin/Agenda Ransomware Safe-Mode Reboot Preparation via bcdedit
id: b8e3d12f-4c65-4b6f-ae2d-qilinsafeboot01
status: experimental
description: Detects bcdedit.exe configuring the next boot for Safe Mode (network or minimal). This is a hallmark TTP of Qilin (Agenda) ransomware operators who reboot victim hosts into Safe Mode where most EDR/AV agents do not load, then trigger encryption.
references:
  - https://www.trendmicro.com/en_us/research/24/g/qilin-ransomware.html
  - https://news.sophos.com/en-us/2024/08/22/qilin-ransomware-caught-stealing-credentials-stored-in-google-chrome/
author: Detection Engineering
date: 2024/09/01
tags:
  - attack.impact
  - attack.t1490
  - attack.t1562.001
  - attack.t1547.001
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_image:
    Image|endswith: '\bcdedit.exe'
  selection_safeboot:
    CommandLine|contains: 'safeboot'
  selection_mode:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'network'
      - 'minimal'
  condition: all of selection_*
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate IT troubleshooting (rare; expect to allowlist by Account/Host once seen)
level: high
ucs.rationale: Qilin / Agenda is one of very few ransomware families that operationally REQUIRES rebooting the victim into Safe Mode to neutralise EDR before detonating its Rust encryptor; affiliates routinely run `bcdedit /set {default} safeboot network` and add their loader as a service under SafeBoot\Network. Generic ransomware detections key on shadow-copy deletion or vssadmin — bcdedit safeboot is far more
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID

ucs.title: [LLM] Qilin (Agenda) Rust-encryptor invocation — operator CLI flags (--password + --no-priv-esc / --no-vm-kill / --target-paths)
ucs.description: Hunts execution of the Qilin/Agenda ransomware encryptor itself. The Rust-rewritten binary (and prior Go variants) is operator-driven and refuses to run without a `--password=<key>` argument, paired with toggles like `--no-priv-esc`, `--no-rename`, `--no-vm-kill`, `--target-files`, `--target-paths`, `--accept-eula` or `--debug`. This combination of flags is essentially unique to Qilin/Agenda among RaaS encryptors and catches the binary regardless of file name or path on disk (operators routinely rename it).
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process_name) as process_name values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent values(Processes.process_hash) as hashes from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process="*--password=*" OR Processes.process="*-password=*" OR Processes.process="*--password *") (Processes.process="*--no-priv-esc*" OR Processes.process="*--no-rename*" OR Processes.process="*--no-vm-kill*" OR Processes.process="*--target-files*" OR Processes.process="*--target-paths*" OR Processes.process="*--accept-eula*" OR Processes.process="*--debug*" OR Processes.process="*-yes *") Processes.user!="*$" by host Processes.user Processes.process Processes.process_path | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime)
ucs.kql:
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
// Qilin / Agenda binary always takes a password argument from the operator
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("--password=", "--password ", "-password=")
// And ALSO at least one of its operator-only toggles
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any (
    "--no-priv-esc", "--no-rename", "--no-vm-kill",
    "--target-files", "--target-paths",
    "--accept-eula", "--debug", "--spread-vm"
  )
| extend HasPassword = ProcessCommandLine has_any ("--password=", "--password ", "-password="),
         QilinFlagHits = countof(ProcessCommandLine, "--no-") + countof(ProcessCommandLine, "--target-") + countof(ProcessCommandLine, "--accept-eula")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, FolderPath, SHA256,
          ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessFileName,
          InitiatingProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          IsRemote = InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("psexec.exe","psexesvc.exe","wmic.exe","powershell.exe","smbexec.py")
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| where CommandLine has_any ("--password=", "--password ", "-password=")
| where CommandLine has_any (
    "--no-priv-esc", "--no-rename", "--no-vm-kill",
    "--target-files", "--target-paths",
    "--accept-eula", "--debug", "--spread-vm"
  )
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, NewProcessName, CommandLine,
          ParentProcessName, TokenElevationType
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Qilin / Agenda Ransomware Encryptor Execution by CLI Flag Pattern
id: a7f2c91e-3b54-4a5e-9d1c-qilinflags001
status: experimental
description: Detects Qilin (Agenda) ransomware encryptor execution by its operator-driven Rust/Go CLI flag combination. The binary requires a --password argument and is typically invoked with one or more of --no-priv-esc, --no-rename, --no-vm-kill, --target-files, --target-paths, --accept-eula, --debug. This combination is essentially unique to Qilin/Agenda and resists binary-renaming evasion.
references:
  - https://www.trendmicro.com/en_us/research/22/h/new-golang-ransomware-agenda.html
  - https://blogs.blackberry.com/en/2023/02/agenda-ransomware-uses-rust-to-target-more-vital-industries
  - https://news.sophos.com/en-us/2024/08/22/qilin-ransomware-caught-stealing-credentials-stored-in-google-chrome/
author: Detection Engineering
date: 2024/09/01
tags:
  - attack.impact
  - attack.t1486
  - attack.t1489
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_password:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - '--password='
      - '-password='
      - '--password '
  selection_qilin_flags:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - '--no-priv-esc'
      - '--no-rename'
      - '--no-vm-kill'
      - '--target-files'
      - '--target-paths'
      - '--accept-eula'
      - '--debug'
      - '--spread-vm'
  filter_machine_acct:
    User|endswith: '$'
  condition: selection_password and selection_qilin_flags and not filter_machine_acct
falsepositives:
  - Authorised red-team / pen-test simulating Agenda or Qilin operator workflows
level: high
ucs.rationale: Qilin/Agenda is operator-piloted RaaS — affiliates rename the encryptor to evade hash/name signatures, but the binary itself enforces a `--password=<key>` argument (the encryption-key bootstrap) and exposes a documented set of toggles (`--no-priv-esc`, `--no-vm-kill`, `--target-paths`, `--accept-eula`) that have been consistent from the Go-based Agenda variant through the Rust-based Qilin / Qilin.
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID
